ModMetadata.Name: Power Transmitter Plus
ModMetadata.Author: SixFive7
ModMetadata.Description:
[h1]Power Transmitter Plus[/h1]
Adds a visible beam, scrolling pulses, a configurable distance cost, and new logic readouts to the Microwave Power Transmitter.
[h2]Features[/h2]
[h3]Visible Laser Beam[/h3] A colored beam is drawn between a linked transmitter and receiver whenever the link is up and powered. The beam is at full brightness at all times while visible, so you can see at a glance which dishes are actually connected.
[h3]Scrolling Pulse Train[/h3] Energy pulses scroll along the beam from transmitter to receiver. Stripe spacing is constant in world meters (same look on a 5m link or a 500m link). Scroll speed scales with power throughput, so a lightly-loaded link is clearly distinguishable from a heavily-loaded one at a glance.
[h3]Distance Cost[/h3] Vanilla Stationeers caps microwave transmission capacity based on distance and hard-stops at 500m. This mod removes that cap entirely and replaces it with a source-draw overhead: the transmitter pulls more from its source cable network per watt delivered, proportional to link distance. Formula: for every watt delivered, the source pulls (1 + k * distance_km) watts. With the default k = 5: [list] [*]0 m: 1.0 watts drawn per watt delivered [*]100 m: 1.5 watts drawn per watt delivered [*]500 m: 3.5 watts drawn per watt delivered [*]1 km: 6.0 watts drawn per watt delivered [*]5 km: 26.0 watts drawn per watt delivered [/list] You can transmit any distance you like, but long-range transmission is paid for in waste heat at the source.
[h3]Logic Readouts[/h3] Five read-only logic types are available on both the transmitter and the receiver, readable from configuration tablets and from IC10: [list] [*][b]MicrowaveSourceDraw[/b] (6571): watts pulled from the source cable network [*][b]MicrowaveDestinationDraw[/b] (6572): watts delivered to the receiver's cable network [*][b]MicrowaveTransmissionLoss[/b] (6573): source minus destination (watts lost to distance) [*][b]MicrowaveEfficiency[/b] (6574): delivered / source as a 0..1 ratio [*][b]MicrowaveLinkedPartner[/b] (6576): ReferenceId of the linked partner dish (0 when unlinked) [/list] The power readouts (6571-6574) return 0 when the link is down, the device is off, or no power is flowing. MicrowaveLinkedPartner returns 0 only when unlinked, regardless of power state.
[h3]Auto-Aim[/h3] A writable logic type [b]MicrowaveAutoAimTarget[/b] (6575) on both transmitter and receiver. Write a Thing's ReferenceId and the dish slews to point at it via the built-in servo; the base game's link raycast decides when the pairing actually forms, so obstacles in the path still take priority. Writing 0 disables. Manually adjusting Horizontal or Vertical cancels auto-aim. This enables IC10 patterns like switching transmit target when remote batteries fill.
[h3]Settings[/h3]
All features are configurable via the mod settings panel.
[b]Client settings[/b] (visual preference, each player sets independently): [list] [*][b]Beam Width[/b] (default 0.1): Thickness of the beam in world meters [*][b]Beam Color[/b] (default 000DFF): Hex RGB color. 000DFF is the vanilla cyan-blue [*][b]Emission Intensity[/b] (default 10.0): HDR brightness multiplier on the beam color [*][b]Stripe Wavelength[/b] (default 2.0): Distance in world meters between one pulse and the next [*][b]Scroll Speed[/b] (default 25.0): Pulse scroll speed in world meters per second at full power (5 kW delivered). Scales with sqrt(intensity) so low loads still move visibly [*][b]Trough Brightness[/b] (default 0.5): Beam brightness between pulses, 0..1. Regenerates on game restart [/list]
[b]Server settings[/b] (the host's value controls gameplay and visuals for everyone): [list] [*][b]Cost Factor (k)[/b] (default 5.0): Per-kilometer overhead on transmitter source draw. k = 0 disables the overhead entirely; k = 10 doubles it compared to the default. Live-broadcast to all connected clients when changed [/list] In multiplayer, the host's beam visual settings (width, color, emission intensity, stripe wavelength, scroll speed) are always broadcast to all clients on connect and on change.
[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[b]Requires:[/b] [list] [*][url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/]BepInEx[/url] [*][url=https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad]StationeersLaunchPad[/url] [/list]
[b]All players[/b] on a server must have Power Transmitter Plus installed. Matching mod versions are enforced during the connection handshake automatically.
[b]Dedicated servers[/b] need the same BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad + PowerTransmitterPlus setup installed server-side. The distance-cost simulation runs server-authoritatively and the handshake rejects mixed installs.
[b]Custom logic type range[/b] 6571-6599 is reserved by this mod. Any other mod that registers a LogicType in that range will collide. The range sits well outside the vanilla enum (0-349) and the range used by Stationeers Logic Extended (1000-1830).
[h2]Reporting Issues[/h2] If you run into a bug or something behaves unexpectedly, please open an issue on [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/issues]GitHub[/url]. Please include the mod name in the title so reports can be triaged. Steam comment notifications don't always come through, so GitHub is the reliable way to make sure a report is seen.
[h2]Credits[/h2] [list] [*][b]ThunderDuck[/b]: Created [url=https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3625190467]Stationeers Logic Extended[/url], which pioneered the pattern for registering custom LogicType values. The injection approach used here (extending ProgrammableChip.AllConstants, Logicable.LogicTypes, and the enum-name lookup paths) is adapted from that work. [/list]
[url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/tree/master/Mods/PowerTransmitterPlus]Source Code on GitHub[/url]
[h2]License[/h2] Licensed under the [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/blob/master/LICENSE]Apache License 2.0[/url]. Attribution required; see the LICENSE and NOTICE files at the repository root.
ModMetadata.ChangeLog:
v1.1.3: Screen syntax highlighting fix - Fixed custom LogicType names rendering red on in-game computer and laptop screen previews
v1.1.2: Remove Shader Name config entry - Removed the Shader Name config entry. The beam shader is now fixed to the built-in fallback chain (Legacy Shaders/Particles/Additive with three fallbacks).
v1.1.1: Patch release (Workshop preview images in build output) - Added to About.xml and ensured Preview.png / thumb.png copy to the build output for the Workshop upload pipeline.
v1.1.0: Link-partner logic and host-broadcast beam visuals - Added MicrowaveLinkedPartner (6576): read-only LogicType returning the ReferenceId of the currently linked partner dish on both transmitter and receiver - Host beam visual settings (width, color, emission intensity, stripe wavelength, scroll speed) are now always synced to all clients in multiplayer
v1.0.0: Initial release - Draws a visible laser beam between linked microwave transmitter and receiver dishes - Scrolling energy pulses along the beam, speed proportional to power throughput - Replaces vanilla distance-based capacity derate with a configurable source-draw overhead (k factor, server-authoritative) - Adds MicrowaveSourceDraw / MicrowaveDestinationDraw / MicrowaveTransmissionLoss / MicrowaveEfficiency logic readouts on both transmitter and receiver (LogicType values 6571-6574) - Writable MicrowaveAutoAimTarget (6575): IC10 can aim the dish at any Thing by ReferenceId - IC10 name resolution for all five new LogicTypes - Full multiplayer support with server-authoritative simulation and live config broadcast
ModMetadata.InGameDescription: Power Transmitter Plus Adds a visible beam, scrolling pulses, a configurable distance cost, and new logic readouts to the Microwave Power Transmitter. Features: - Visible laser beam between linked transmitter and receiver dishes - Scrolling energy pulses, speed scales with power throughput - Replaces vanilla distance derate with a source-draw overhead (1 + k * distance_km watts drawn per watt delivered). Default k = 5. No more 500m hard cap. - Five read-only logic readouts (tablet + IC10): MicrowaveSourceDraw (6571), MicrowaveDestinationDraw (6572), MicrowaveTransmissionLoss (6573), MicrowaveEfficiency (6574), MicrowaveLinkedPartner (6576) - Writable MicrowaveAutoAimTarget (6575): write a Thing's ReferenceId to auto-aim the dish; write 0 to disable - Full multiplayer support with server-authoritative simulation Settings: All features are configurable via the mod settings panel. Server setting Cost Factor (k) controls gameplay for all players. In multiplayer, the host's beam visual settings always override client-local config. Requirements: BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad Multiplayer: All players must have this mod installed. Matching mod versions are enforced during the connection handshake automatically. Dedicated servers need the same setup installed server-side.
